original_hypothesis.title: Titn Constructor Mints Initial Supply to msg.sender Instead of Designated Owner
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The Titn constructor calls _mint(msg.sender, initialMintAmount) while setting the owner to _delegate via Ownable(_delegate). If the deployer (msg.sender) is a different address from _delegate, the entire initial supply (1B TITN on Base) goes to the deployer rather than the intended owner. In the current deployment scripts, deployer == _delegate, but the code does not enforce this assumption. A deployment via a factory contract, multisig, or CREATE2 proxy where msg.sender != _delegate would result in supply misattribution.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The constructor mints to msg.sender but assigns ownership to _delegate. These could be different addresses depending on deployment method. On Base, 1B TITN is minted at construction — if this goes to the wrong address, the entire protocol supply is compromised.
deployment_assumptions: msg.sender == _delegate during deployment (true in current scripts, but not enforced in code)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Titn.ts: deployer is used as both msg.sender and _delegate argument. But Titn.sol constructor does not assert(msg.sender == _delegate).

proof_of_signal.pattern: _mint(msg.sender, initialMintAmount) in constructor while Ownable(_delegate) sets owner to _delegate. No assertion that msg.sender == _delegate.

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Deploy via factory where msg.sender != intended owner → 1B TITN goes to factory
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Deployer (if not owner) receives entire initial supply

economic_questions: Was the deployment performed directly by the deployer EOA or via a factory/proxy?
Is the current deployer address the same as the owner?
Can the deployment be replayed with a different deployment method?
validation_requirements: Verify on-chain that deployer == owner for both Base and Arbitrum deployments
Check if any future redeployment plan uses factory contracts
Confirm msg.sender receives initial mint, not _delegate

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 1B TITN if deployed incorrectly

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All protocol participants if supply goes to wrong address

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify on-chain deployment parameters match script expectations

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
